shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Cards and chart containers at rest. Detaches the surface from the background without lifting it.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Data tooltip only. The sole floating element above the page plane.

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Default for color state transitions (toggle active/inactive, row hover). Should feel immediate.

components.name: Filter Toggle (Active)
components.description: Selected position or year filter. Blue-600 background, white text. The only use of the primary accent on interactive controls.
components.css: .ds-toggle-active { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 4px 12px; background-color: #2563eb; color: #ffffff; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 4px; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-toggle-active:hover { background-color: #1d4ed8; } .ds-toggle-active:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Filter Toggle (Inactive)
components.description: Unselected position or year filter. Muted background, low visual weight. Activates on click.
components.css: .ds-toggle-inactive { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 4px 12px; background-color: #f3f4f6; color: #4b5563; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 4px; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-toggle-inactive:hover { background-color: #e5e7eb; } .ds-toggle-inactive:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2563eb; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Summary Card
components.description: Single-metric summary tile. Label over value. Used in the 4-across grid above the chart. No icon, no decoration — the number is the card.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); } .ds-card-label { margin: 0; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #9ca3af; } .ds-card-value { margin: 4px 0 0; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.2; color: #111827; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Table Header Cell
components.description: Sticky column header with sort affordance. Uppercase tracked label, sort arrow indicator. Active sort shifts text to ink.
components.css: .ds-th { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10; background-color: #f9fafb; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #6b7280; text-align: right; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-th:hover { background-color: #f3f4f6; } .ds-th--active { color: #111827; } .ds-sort { margin-left: 4px; }

components.name: Table Row
components.description: Data row. Hairline bottom border. Hover tints the entire row with the primary accent at low opacity — the only row-level use of blue.
components.html: Victor Wembanyama7'5¼"+4.1"
components.css: .ds-row { border-bottom: 1px solid #f3f4f6; transition: background-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-row:hover { background-color: rgba(239, 246, 255, 0.4); } .ds-td { padding: 8px 12px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; text-align: right; } .ds-td--name { text-align: left; font-weight: 500; color: #111827; } .ds-td--true-size { font-weight: 600; color: #2563eb; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-td--signal { font-weight: 500; color: #9ca3af; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-td--positive { color: #16a34a; } .ds-td--negative { color: #ef4444; }

components.name: Data Tooltip
components.description: Hover tooltip on scatter plot dots. Floating surface — the only shadow-lg element in the system. Structured identically to the table: label left, value right, tabular-nums throughout.
components.html:
Victor Wembanyama
C
Height7'4"
Wingspan8'0"
True Size7'5¼"
components.css: .ds-tooltip { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 4px 6px -4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1); font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.5; min-width: 160px; } .ds-tooltip-name { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: #111827; margin-bottom: 2px; } .ds-tooltip-pos { color: #9ca3af; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-tooltip-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; gap: 24px; } .ds-tooltip-row--accent { border-top: 1px solid #f3f4f6; padding-top: 4px; margin-top: 4px; } .ds-tooltip-val { font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-tooltip-val--accent { font-weight: 600; color: #2563eb; }

narrative.overview:
The combine floor is a room of pure measurement. No performance, no narrative — just bodies in space and numbers that tell the truth. True Size's design system carries that same discipline into every screen: dense but legible, exact without being cold, functional without being bare.

Everything the interface shows is load-bearing. The gray hierarchy exists to let numbers register at a glance. The single blue accent marks exactly one thing — the metric this tool was built around. Components don't animate to show off; state changes are immediate and legible. The tool should disappear into the task of understanding a prospect's physical profile.

This system explicitly rejects the aesthetic of its category's worst reflexes: the red-and-black energy of ESPN, the navy-and-bar-chart of enterprise SaaS dashboards, the gamified badge-and-leaderboard rhythm of fantasy apps. Those surfaces shout to be noticed. This one assumes the user already knows what they're looking for.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained single-accent palette — blue-600 used only as a functional indicator, never decoration
Fixed rem typography scale — product UI consistency, no fluid headings
Dense information layout — tabular data at full width, compact row height, no padding theater
Flat surfaces with structural borders — depth through tonal layers, not shadows
Semantic color reserved for data signals — green/red for quartile thresholds only

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Blue-600 is the only true accent. It appears on ≤15% of any given screen. A second accent color is prohibited — it would split the visual priority system that makes True Size values instantly identifiable.

rules.name: The Semantic Firewall Rule
rules.body: Green (#16a34a) and red (#ef4444) appear only as quartile signal colors on wingspan gap and vs. actual columns. Never applied to UI state, decorative highlights, or success/error messaging.

rules.name: The Tabular Invariant Rule
rules.body: Every number displayed in a data context — table cells, card values, tooltip measurements — uses font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. No exceptions. Columns that don't align are broken.

rules.name: The Label Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracking is reserved for short categorical labels (≤4 words). Never applied to sentences, player names, or any prose copy.

rules.name: The Flat Plane Rule
rules.body: Borders and tonal steps handle structural separation. Shadow appears on two surfaces only: cards at rest (resting) and the data tooltip (floating). Adding shadow to anything else means the structure is wrong — fix the layout instead.

dos: Do use #2563eb as the sole accent — active toggles, True Size values, and row hover tint only.
Do apply tabular-nums to every numeric value in a data context, without exception.
Do use uppercase tracked labels for categorical labels of ≤4 words. Nothing longer.
Do use #e5e7eb as the single structural border token across the entire surface.
Do keep elevation flat — resting shadow on cards, floating shadow on the tooltip, nothing else.
Do use #16a34a and #ef4444 strictly for quartile signal columns.
Do right-align all numeric columns. Left-align player names only.
Do keep transitions to transition-colors at 150ms. State changes should feel immediate, not choreographed.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color. The One Accent Rule is what makes True Size values visually unambiguous at a glance.
Don't use green or red for UI state, decorative highlights, or success/error messages. The Semantic Firewall Rule is absolute.
Don't import a display or custom typeface for UI labels, buttons, or table headers.
Don't add shadows beyond the resting card shadow and the floating tooltip shadow.
Don't apply uppercase tracking to body copy, player names, or text longer than 4 words.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or list items.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). Ever.
Don't introduce glassmorphism, hero-metric templates, or identical card grids.
Don't build toward ESPN — loud colors, score-ticker energy, red-and-black aggression.
Don't gamify. No badges, no leaderboards, no achievement states. The data is the reward.
